description: Address code review findings: log notification scheduling errors, add missing tests for /admin/exam/{exam_id} auth and notification insertion, add startup warning for default SECRET_KEY.
steps: In app/routers/exam.py, change the 'except Exception: pass' around the follow-up notification insert to log the error instead of silently swallowing it.
In tests/test_app.py, add a test that accessing /admin/exam/1 without login redirects to /admin/login.
In tests/test_app.py, add a test that after a failed exam submission, a follow_up_7day row exists in the notifications table.
In main.py, after startup diagnostics, add a warning if settings.SECRET_KEY is the default value.
acceptance_criteria: The except block around follow-up notification insert prints a descriptive error message instead of silently passing
A test exists verifying /admin/exam/{exam_id} requires auth
A test exists verifying follow_up_7day notification is created for failed candidates
A startup warning is printed if SECRET_KEY has the default value
findings: All 37 tests pass (24 in test_app.py, 13 in other test files). The notification test creates a candidate, starts an exam, inserts a single question, submits a wrong answer, and verifies the follow_up_7day row is present. The auth test for /admin/exam/1 works because require_admin redirects before attempting to fetch the non-existent exam from the DB.
